AAD.GroupName: Strong Authentication and a Secure Registration Process

Controls.Requirement: The Authentication Methods Manage Migration feature SHALL be set to Migration Complete.
Managed devices SHOULD be required for authentication.
Controls.Details: 0 conditional access policy(s) found that meet(s) all requirements.
Controls.OriginalDetails: 0 conditional access policy(s) found that meet(s) all requirements. View all CA policies.

Controls.Requirement: Security logs SHALL be sent to the agency's security operations center for monitoring.
Controls.Details: This product does not currently have the capability to check compliance for this policy. See Secure Configuration Baseline policy, https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGear/blob/v1.7.1/PowerShell/ScubaGear/baselines/aad.md#msaad41v1 for instructions on manual check
Controls.OriginalDetails: This product does not currently have the capability to check compliance for this policy. See Secure Configuration Baseline policy for instructions on manual check

Controls.Requirement: All users SHALL be added to Exchange Online Protection (EOP) in either the standard or strict preset security policy.
Domain impersonation protection SHOULD be enabled for domains owned by the agency in both the standard and strict preset policies.
Controls.Details: No agency domains defined for impersonation protection assessment. See configuration file documentation for details on how to define.
Controls.OriginalDetails: No agency domains defined for impersonation protection assessment. See configuration file documentation for details on how to define.

Controls.Requirement: Automatic forwarding to external domains SHALL be disabled.
Controls.Details: 1 remote domain allows automatic forwarding: *. NOTE: specific domains that have a legitimate need to allow remote forwarding can be configured in a ScubaGear config file.
Controls.OriginalDetails: 1 remote domain allows automatic forwarding: *. NOTE: specific domains that have a legitimate need to allow remote forwarding can be configured in a ScubaGear config file.

Controls.Requirement: An SPF policy SHALL be published for each domain that fails all non-approved senders.
Controls.Details: Requirement met. View DNS logs, #dns-logs for more details.
Controls.OriginalDetails: Requirement met. View DNS logs for more details.

Controls.Requirement: DKIM SHOULD be enabled for all domains.
Controls.Details: 1 agency domain(s) found in violation: TestTenant.onmicrosoft.com. View DNS logs, #dns-logs for more details.

Controls.Requirement: A DLP solution SHALL be used.
Controls.Details: A custom product can be used to fulfill this policy requirement. If a custom product is used, a 3rd party assessment tool or manual review is needed to ensure compliance. If you are using Defender for Office 365 to implement this policy, ensure when running ScubaGear that 'defender' is an argument to the -ProductNames parameter. Then, review the corresponding Defender policy that fulfills the requirements of this policy on the Defender ScubaGear HTML report. See the Secure Configuration Baseline policy, https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGear/blob/v1.7.1/PowerShell/ScubaGear/baselines/exo.md#msexo81v2 for instructions on a manual check.

Controls.Requirement: The ability to create production and sandbox environments SHALL be restricted to admins.
External sharing for SharePoint SHALL be limited to "Existing guests" or "Only people in your organization".
Anonymous users SHALL NOT be enabled to start meetings.
External access for users SHALL only be enabled on a per-domain basis.
Controls.Details: 1 meeting policy(ies) that allow external access across all domains: Global
Controls.OriginalDetails: 1 meeting policy(ies) that allow external access across all domains: Global
